evals.prompt:
Execute the execution flow for the SSO feature. The project is a Go multi-tenant backend. Here are the input artifacts:

- Execution backlog: evals/fixtures/sso-wave1-backlog.md
- Codebase to modify: evals/fixtures/sso-codebase/
- Supporting design context: evals/fixtures/sso-stage5-artifacts/implementation-design.md
- Design decisions: evals/fixtures/sso-stage5-artifacts/design-decisions.md
- System analysis: evals/fixtures/sso-stage5-artifacts/system-analysis.md
- Constraints and risks: evals/fixtures/sso-stage5-artifacts/constraints-risks-analysis.md

Execute all 3 foundation subtasks (ST-1, ST-2, ST-3). They are all independent and can run in parallel. Follow the execution flow process: dispatch implementers, run task review and code review for each, close only after both reviews pass.
evals.expected_output: All 3 subtasks implemented with correct code changes, execution-status.md tracking file created and maintained, dual review (task + code) executed for each subtask
expectations: execution-status.md artifact is created and tracks all 3 subtasks
ST-1: migrations/002_sso.up.sql created with tenant_sso_config table and users.keycloak_id column
ST-1: migrations/002_sso.down.sql created with reverse operations
ST-2: config/config.go modified to include SSOConfig struct with Keycloak fields
ST-3: internal/tenant/model.go modified to include TenantSSOConfig struct
ST-3: internal/user/model.go modified to include KeycloakID *string field
Task review was performed for each subtask (task-reviewer agent was spawned)
Code review was performed for each subtask (code-reviewer agent was spawned)
Implementer prompts include detailed design context from implementation-design.md (mandatory, not optional)
Implementer prompts include system analysis context from system-analysis.md for the relevant modules
Implementer prompts include concrete constraint details with file paths and data formats, not one-line summaries

evals.prompt:
I have a simple Go web app at evals/fixtures/sso-codebase/ and I need you to implement these tasks using the execution flow skill:

(tasks are in evals/fixtures/raw-tasks.md)

No formal planning documents — just these two tasks. Use the execution flow to implement them with proper review gates.
evals.expected_output: Both tasks implemented with correct code, execution tracking artifacts created, tasks normalized from raw format, dual review executed for each
expectations: Raw tasks from raw-tasks.md are normalized into structured subtasks before execution
Health check endpoint implementation exists (handler returning JSON status)
Health check route is registered in main.go
Logging middleware exists in internal/middleware/logging.go or similar path
Middleware logs method, path, status code, and duration
Middleware is applied to the router in main.go
Task review was performed for each task
Code review was performed for each task
execution-status.md tracking artifact is created
